scenario.context: Station 4 of the daily run: assess threats and social engineering.
scenario.mission: You identify current threats and define safe handling of social engineering situations.

station.profile: Phishing, identity abuse, and suspicious contacts

customerRequest.subject: Station 04: secure threat landscape and social engineering
customerRequest.body: Please assess typical threats for the sales area and formulate a short action recommendation for handling social engineering attempts.

questions.title: Typical warning signal for phishing
questions.prompt: Which characteristic is a particularly strong warning sign for phishing?

options.text: Time pressure plus a request to reveal credentials immediately
options.rationale: This exact mix is often used for manipulation.

options.text: A properly verified call-back via known contact channels
A normal internal meeting notice without a data request
options.rationale: This alone is not a strong warning sign.

feedback.correct: Correct: Time pressure and data requests are strong warning signs.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: pay more attention to manipulation patterns.

questions.title: Relevant threats in daily work
questions.prompt: Which threats are relevant for a typical sales workstation? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Phishing for mail or CRM accounts
options.rationale: Accounts are a central attack target.

options.text: Misconfigured permissions with overly broad access rights

options.rationale: This can prepare malware or identity theft.
That is not an IT threat.
This is a typical entry point for social engineering.

feedback.correct: Correct: You connect the threat landscape with the workstation context.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: consider accounts, permissions, and suspicious contacts more strongly.

questions.title: Handling a suspicious request
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for safe handling.
items: Submit the security incident via the reporting path
Critically review the request and do not release sensitive data
Verify identity via known contact channels
Document the incident and align feedback
Implement further measures according to policy
correctOrder: Critically review the request and do not release sensitive data
Verify identity via known contact channels
Submit the security incident via the reporting path
Implement further measures according to policy
Document the incident and align feedback

feedback.correct: Correct: The flow protects data and stays process-compliant.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: sequence verification, reporting, and documentation clearly.

questions.title: Risk score of a threat
questions.prompt: With likelihood 3 and impact 4: risk score = likelihood * impact. What is the score?

questions.title: Team note on social engineering
questions.prompt: Formulate in 4-6 sentences a team note with warning signal, verification rule, and reporting path.
questions.expectedOutline: Name the warning signal, state the safe behavior rule, and clarify the reporting path.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is clear, preventive, and usable.
feedback.wrong: Please state warning signal, verification, and reporting path more clearly.
